understand.plain: The function tob converts an integer b into its binary representation stored in an array (LSB first). The function pp computes a^b using the binary representation of b (binary exponentiation / fast power). Each bit of b tells whether to multiply the current power of a into the total.

keywords.explain: A method to compute a^b efficiently by iterating over bits of b. For each set bit at position i, multiply tot by a^(2^i).
keywords.example: 3^4: binary of 4 is 100, only bit 2 is set, so tot = 3^4 = 81

keywords.explain: Converts integer b to binary, storing bits LSB-first in arr[]. Returns the number of bits.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='480' height='220'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='480' height='220' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)'/><text x='20' y='30' class='lbl ink'>pp(3, 4): binary of 4 = 100 (LSB first: [0,0,1])</text><rect x='20' y='50' width='60' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='50' y='76' class='lbl mut' text-anchor='middle'>arr[0]=0</text><rect x='100' y='50' width='60' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='130' y='76' class='lbl mut' text-anchor='middle'>arr[1]=0</text><rect x='180' y='50' width='60' height='40' class='hi'/><text x='210' y='76' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>arr[2]=1</text><text x='20' y='130' class='sub ink'>i=0: arr[0]=0, ex=3, skip mult. ex=3*3=9</text><text x='20' y='155' class='sub ink'>i=1: arr[1]=0, ex=9, skip mult. ex=9*9=81</text><text x='20' y='180' class='sub ink'>i=2: arr[2]=1, tot=1*81=81. ex=81*81</text><text x='20' y='205' class='lbl ink'>Result: pp(3,4) = 3^4 = 81</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Trace of pp(3,4): binary of 4 is [0,0,1] LSB-first. Only bit index 2 is set, contributing ex=3^4=81 to tot. Final result is 81.

terms.connects: ex starts as 3 and is squared each iteration: 3, 9, 81, ...

terms.meaning: The exponent; its binary form drives which powers of a to multiply
terms.connects: tob(4, arr) fills arr=[0,0,1] and returns len=3

terms.meaning: Accumulates the result by multiplying ex whenever arr[i]=1

given.plan: Trace tob(4, arr) to get the binary digits of 4 (LSB first), then trace the pp loop: for each bit, if set multiply tot by ex, then always square ex. The result is a^b computed by binary exponentiation.

steps.title: Trace tob(4, arr)
steps.formula_raw: b % 2 gives LSB; b = b / 2 shifts right
steps.apply:
i=0: 4%2=0, arr[0]=0, b=2
i=1: 2%2=0, arr[1]=0, b=1
i=2: 1%2=1, arr[2]=1, b=0 -> loop ends
Returns len=3. So arr=[0,0,1] (LSB-first binary of 4).
steps.note: arr stores bits of b from LSB to MSB. 4 = 100 in binary, so arr=[0,0,1].

steps.title: Trace pp loop
steps.formula_raw: tot *= ex when arr[i]==1; ex = ex * ex each iteration
steps.apply:
ex=3, tot=1, len=3
i=0: arr[0]=0, skip multiply. ex=3*3=9
i=1: arr[1]=0, skip multiply. ex=9*9=81
i=2: arr[2]=1, tot=1*81=81. ex=81*81 (not used)
Return tot = 81

solution.result: The value returned by pp(3, 4) is 81. Answer: A. 81

questions.formula_note: Binary exponentiation (fast power): compute a^b in O(log b) multiplications by iterating over bits of b.

questions.to_find: The value returned by SimpleFunction(Z, 10, 2) where Z[i]=1 for all i, n=10, x=2

understand.plain: SimpleFunction implements Horner's method for evaluating a polynomial. Given an array Y of coefficients and a value x, it computes Y[0]*x^(n-1) + Y[1]*x^(n-2) + ... + Y[n-1]. With all coefficients = 1 and x = 2, this is 2^9 + 2^8 + ... + 2^0 = 2^10 - 1 = 1023.

keywords.explain: An efficient algorithm to evaluate polynomials by nesting multiplications: ((Y[0]*x + Y[1])*x + Y[2])... avoids computing powers separately.
Sum of powers of a constant: 1 + r + r^2 + ... + r^(n-1) = (r^n - 1)/(r-1)

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='480' height='160'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='480' height='160' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)'/><text x='20' y='30' class='lbl ink'>Horner's method: Z all 1s, x=2, n=10</text><text x='20' y='60' class='sub ink'>total = 1</text><text x='20' y='80' class='sub ink'>After loop: total = 2^9 + 2^8 + ... + 2^1 + 2^0</text><text x='20' y='100' class='sub ink'>= sum_{i=0}^{9} 2^i = 2^10 - 1 = 1023</text><rect x='20' y='115' width='200' height='35' class='hi'/><text x='120' y='137' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>Result = 1023</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Horner's method applied to array of 10 ones with x=2: equivalent to evaluating 2^9 + 2^8 + ... + 2^0 = 1023.

given.aim: Find what polynomial SimpleFunction evaluates and compute SimpleFunction(Z, 10, 2) with Z[i]=1

terms.meaning: Array length; polynomial has degree n-1 = 9
terms.example: Loop runs from loopIndex=1 to 9 (9 iterations)
terms.connects: Each iteration applies one level of Horner nesting

terms.meaning: The point at which the polynomial is evaluated

given.plan: Recognize SimpleFunction as Horner's method. With all Z[i]=1 and x=2, the result is the sum of a geometric series: 1 + 2 + 4 + ... + 2^9 = 2^10 - 1 = 1023.

steps.title: Identify the polynomial being evaluated
steps.apply:
Horner's method: start with total=Y[0]=1, then for each step: total = x*total + Y[i].
With all Y[i]=1: final total = 1*2^9 + 1*2^8 + ... + 1*2^0 = sum_{k=0}^{9} 2^k
steps.note: Horner's method evaluates an n-1 degree polynomial in n-1 multiplications.

solution.result: The value returned by SimpleFunction(Z, 10, 2) is 1023. Answer: A. 1023

questions.formula_note: Horner's method for polynomial evaluation; geometric series sum: sum_{k=0}^{n-1} r^k = (r^n - 1)/(r-1).

understand.plain: SomeFunction implements the Euclidean algorithm for computing the Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of two numbers using repeated subtraction. When two numbers are equal it returns that number; when unequal it subtracts the smaller from the larger recursively. GCD(15, 255) = 15 since 255 = 15 * 17.

keywords.explain: The largest integer that divides both x and y without a remainder.
Repeatedly subtract the smaller number from the larger. When both are equal the result is their GCD.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='480' height='180'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='480' height='180' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)'/><text x='20' y='30' class='lbl ink'>SomeFunction(15, 255) = GCD(15, 255)</text><text x='20' y='58' class='sub ink'>255 = 15 * 17, so 15 divides 255 exactly.</text><text x='20' y='80' class='sub ink'>Subtraction steps: 255-15=240, 240-15=225, ..., 30-15=15</text><text x='20' y='100' class='sub ink'>After 17 subtractions: x=15, y=15 -> return x = 15</text><rect x='20' y='118' width='200' height='40' class='hi'/><text x='120' y='143' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>GCD(15, 255) = 15</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Euclidean subtraction: SomeFunction(15, 255) reduces 255 by 15 repeatedly (17 times) until both equal 15, returning 15.

given.aim: Identify what SomeFunction computes and evaluate SomeFunction(15, 255)

terms.meaning: The two inputs; we need to find their GCD
terms.connects: The subtraction loop will reduce y by 15 each call until x == y

terms.meaning: When both numbers are equal, return x (= their GCD)
terms.connects: Termination condition of the Euclidean subtraction algorithm

terms.term: Recursive case: x > y or y > x
terms.meaning: Subtract smaller from larger, preserving the GCD
terms.connects: GCD(a, b) = GCD(a-b, b) when a > b is the mathematical basis

given.plan: Recognize the subtraction-based Euclidean algorithm. Since 15 divides 255 (255 = 15*17), the function will subtract 15 from 255 repeatedly until both are 15, then return 15.

steps.title: Identify the function
steps.formula_raw: GCD(x, y) = GCD(x - y, y) if x > y; GCD(x, y) = x if x == y
steps.apply:
The recursion matches the subtraction form of the Euclidean GCD algorithm:
- Returns 1 if either argument is 1
- Returns x when x == y (GCD found)
- Subtracts smaller from larger otherwise
steps.note: SomeFunction(x, y) = GCD(x, y) for positive integers.

steps.title: Compute GCD(15, 255)
steps.formula_raw: GCD(a, b) = GCD(a, b - a) when b > a
steps.note: Since 15 divides 255 evenly, the GCD is 15 itself.

solution.result: The value returned by SomeFunction(15, 255) is 15. Answer: B. 15

questions.formula_note: Euclidean algorithm (subtraction form): GCD(a, b) = GCD(a-b, b) when a > b; GCD(a, a) = a.
